2 | N3 Multiplication, Division and their Applications | Knowledge Organiser | 2 | Knowledge | Answer | 1 | What is multiplication? | Repeated addition of the same number. | 2 | What is division? | Splitting a number into equal parts. | 3 | What is meant by metric measurements? | A decimal system of weights and measures. | 4 | What is a product? | The result of multiplying two or more numbers. | 5 | What are decimals? | Numbers between whole numbers, expressed with a decimal point. | 6 | What is the area? | The space inside a shape. | 7 | What is the mean of a data set? | The average value of the data. | 8 | What is a square number? | A number that is the product of an integer multiplied by itself. | 9 | What is a cube number? | A number that is the product of an integer multiplied by itself twice. | 10 | What is a square root? | A value that, when squared, gives the original number. | 11 | What is a cube root? | A value that, when cubed, gives the original number. | 12 | What is an operation? | A mathematical action like addition, subtraction, multiplication or division. | 13 | What is a multiple? | The result of multiplying a number by an integer. | 14 | What is a lowest common multiple? | The smallest multiple shared by two or more integers. | 15 | What is a factor? | A number that divides exactly into another number. | 16 | What is a highest common factor? | The largest factor shared by two or more integers. | 17 | What is a prime number? | A positive integer with exactly two factors. | 18 | What is a composite number? | A number with more than two factors.

This is a | division problem | . | Introduction – read and annotate: | Multiplying and dividing by powers of 10 means working with numbers like 10, 100, 1,000, and so | on. Being able to do this helps you solve related calculations more easily. For example, if you know | that 3 × 4 = 12, then you can use that fact to work out 30 × 4 by | recognising | that 3 has been | multiplied by 10—so there's no need to do the full calculation again. When you multiply by a power | of 10, the digits move to the left, making the number bigger; when you divide by a power of 10, the | digits move to the right, making the number smaller.
